mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses more than 0.3 percentage points above its trailing 12-month low, labor-market deterioration signals weaker earnings growth and higher recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its trailing 12-month low by > 0.3pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not have lower mean 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if the downside hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month RSAFS growth combined with FEDFUNDS below 3% indicates consumer demand growth without restrictive policy pressure, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: consumer-led expansion with moderate policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition produces lower average 20-day returns than periods with weak RSAFS growth, or if interaction term t-statistic is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in INDPRO signals improving real output and operating leverage for listed firms, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends have a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month average by more than 10%, housing demand weakness transmits to construction, durable goods, and credit sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month average by > 10%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST weakness is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL acceleration above 4% annualized while FEDFUNDS is rising creates an inflation-tightening shock that compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 4% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-tightening trigger fails to lower average 5-day returns relative to baseline, or if negative-return frequency is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth over 3 months with UNRATE at or below 4% signals a tight but still expanding labor market that supports income growth and consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have lower 20-day returns than non-triggered observations, or if the PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction coefficient is nonpositive.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth with positive RSAFS momentum confirms broad real demand and household spending strength, improving earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0 and RSAFS 3-month growth > 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-demand trigger has a 60-day average return no higher than unconditional returns, or if its walk-forward hit rate is below 52%.
